subheadline: Zergboard gives you a Trello-simple interface with Linear-level structure, multi-tenant workspaces, and an API-first core so your agent flows can create, update, and resolve work automatically.
value_proposition: A lightweight kanban board with multi-tenant workspaces and an API-first design built for teams and AI agent automation.
target_audience: Small-to-mid-size product and engineering teams, and developers building agent-driven workflows who want structure without enterprise overhead.

pricing.price_anchoring: Free tier lowers entry barrier; annual billing framed as saving ~15%; tier names (Free → Core → Growth → Scale) imply natural progression rather than hard upsell

features: Kanban cards and columns with fast drag-and-drop
Multi-tenant workspaces with membership-based access control
API-first architecture for automation, bots, and agents
Webhooks and API tokens (Growth tier and above)
differentiators: Trello-simple interface paired with Linear-level structure
Multi-tenant by default — not bolted on
API-first core explicitly designed for agent and bot workflows
Deploy-ready on Fly.io with self-host option (Nuxt + Postgres)
No enterprise bloat — explicit positioning against heavyweight tools

design_patterns.layout: Single-page vertical scroll: hero → live snapshot widget → docs callout → feature grid → pricing table → FAQ
design_patterns.color_scheme: Implied minimal/neutral with functional UI emphasis; no strong color signals from text alone
design_patterns.typography: Clean, modern sans-serif implied by terse, punchy copy blocks
design_patterns.visual_style: Minimal and technical with a developer-first sensibility
notable_patterns: Live work snapshot widget simulating real-time kanban activity
Comparison framing (Trello-simple + Linear-level) as positioning shorthand

emotional_tone: confident and pragmatic — targets builders who are allergic to bloat and want something that just works
key_objections_addressed: Can AI agents use this? — Yes, API is intentionally minimal and stable
Does it handle multiple clients or tenants? — Yes, orgs are fully isolated
Am I locked in if I want to self-host later? — No, portable Nuxt + Postgres stack
Is there a free way to try it? — Yes, up to 3 users at no cost
missing_or_weak: Zero social proof — no customer logos, testimonials, user counts, or press mentions
No case study or concrete example of agent integration in production
No security or compliance signals (SOC 2, GDPR, encryption at rest)
Live snapshot widget reads as a demo animation, not real data — misses opportunity to show genuine traction
No named customers or recognizable brand associations to build credibility
Scale tier says 'Talk to sales' but there's no visible way to contact sales on the page
No onboarding flow preview or screenshots of the actual product UI
Annual discount framed vaguely ('roughly 15%') rather than shown as a concrete dollar save
standout_elements: Crisp competitive positioning using two known brands (Trello + Linear) as reference points — immediately frames the product without lengthy explanation
Agent/automation angle is front and center, not buried — smart differentiation as AI workflows become mainstream
FAQ directly addresses the four most likely objections for this category (agents, multi-tenancy, self-hosting, free tier)
Transparent four-tier pricing on the landing page with per-seat clarity
Self-host option signals long-term trust and avoids vendor lock-in anxiety for technical buyers
